title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are a natural and effective way to combat halitosis, or chronic bad breath. These teas contain antioxidants and antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth and freshen breath. Examples of herbal teas that aid in bad breath relief include peppermint, chamomile, and fennel tea. Drinking these teas can improve one's quality of life by boosting confidence and reducing social anxiety related to bad breath.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal teas is to drink a cup of peppermint tea after meals. Peppermint's antibacterial properties help reduce bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Another option is to try sage tea, which has natural decongestant properties that can help dry up excess saliva and kill bacteria. Both teas are easy to find and brew at home.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, teas like fennel and dill have carminative properties that can relieve bloating and gas, providing relief from discomfort and unpleasant odors.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the bacteria that can cause halitosis. The menthol in peppermint also has a freshening effect on the mouth and tongue, leaving your breath smelling clean and pure. Additionally, peppermint tea's soothing qualities can help to calm an upset stomach, which is often linked to bad breath. By drinking herbal peppermint tea regularly, you can enjoy fresh, clean breath all day long.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tea contains antimicrobial compounds that can help kill bacteria in the mouth, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that cause bad breath.

Digestive aid: Peppermint tea can help soothe digestive issues such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the amount of saliva produced, which helps neutralize acids in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth tissues, which can contribute to bad breath and gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the mouth and freshening breath.
Buds: Peppermint buds contain menthol, which helps to eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath and also has a refreshing and calming effect on the oral mucosa.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves into a heat-resistant teapot or mug.
Bring 8 ounces of boiling water to a simmer and pour it over the peppermint leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing the herbal oils and flavonoids.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately, taking small sips as needed to alleviate bad breath and freshen your mouth.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Kansas Coneflower tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various oral pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, which can cause bad breath by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the mouth.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the gums, tongue, and cheeks, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing swelling and discomfort in these areas.

Antioxidant Properties: Kansas Coneflower tea contains antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals in the mouth, which can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties, roots help combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, seeds soothe gum inflammation and reduce bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia root, eight ounces of boiling water, and a teapot.
Measure the echinacea angustifolia root and add it to the boiling water in the teapot, allowing it to steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Add one teaspoon of raw honey to the tea, stirring well to dissolve and sweeten to taste, taking two cups three times daily.
Prepare fresh herbal tea as needed, storing any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate the underlying causes of halitosis. The tea's active compounds, such as echinacea and other flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath, leaving your mouth feeling fresh and clean. Additionally, coneflower teas have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm gum inflammation, further reducing odors and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Lemon balm teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath. The tea's antiseptic compounds also help to reduce plaque and inflammation in the mouth, further combating halitosis. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating that often contribute to bad breath. remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for the desired serving size.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water and add it to a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or according to the package instructions for maximum potency.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Serve the tea hot or chilled as desired, ideally after meals or at bedtime to reduce morning breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties. Leaves: Thyme leaves contain essential oils that have a natural antibacterial and antifungal effect, helping to eliminate the causes of bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Boil one cup of water and add the thyme leaves for five minutes to steep.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth immediately after steeping.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to room temperature within thirty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively eliminate bacteria that cause halitosis. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause bad breath by reducing their growth and preventing them from producing volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that contribute to bad breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves or purchase dried ones in adequate quantity for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried origanum vulgare leaves or three teaspoons of fresh leaves per eight ounces water.
Bring the measured water to a boil and then reduce the heat to simmer for five minutes to prepare the infusion.
Steep the origanum vulgare leaves in the hot water for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discard the solids, and drink immediately or store in the refrigerator for up to two days.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids after three to four minutes.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before drinking it, taking up to two cups per day as needed for fresh breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with bad breath because its unique composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenes has natural antibacterial properties that effectively combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to dry out the mouth, reducing the amount of bacteria that can thrive and cause bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ginkgo biloba tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help combat the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba tea can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which may contribute to bad breath, by reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of Ginkgo biloba tea helps neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath, while also promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help reduce bacterial growth and eliminate odors, making them effective in treating bad breath.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe mouth and gum inflammation, which is a common cause of bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured ginkgo biloba leaves to the hot water and steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool for at least thirty minutes before consuming it.
